channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: The hidden battlefield: Censorship in the Israel–Iran war
item.description: Missiles fly between Israel and Iran, but censorship and access limits mean much of the war remains hidden from view.

item.title: Ship crew film drone strike on oil facility at Oman’s Salalah port
item.description: Crew on-board a nearby vessel at Oman’s Salalah port filmed the moment a drone struck the oil facility.

item.title: What’s left in Iran ‘could be taken out in an hour’, says Trump
item.description: President Trump has told reporters that the US has destroyed Iran’s navy, anti-aircraft systems, radars and leadership.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: US war on Iran, Mexico violence, visa bans, Iraq qualifier
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 was billed as one of the easiest to organise, but challenges still lie in wait.

item.title: Trump says Iran war to end ‘soon’ as Israel claims no time limit
item.description: US president hails war progress as Iran continues to block Hormuz Strait and launch attacks across the region.

item.title: IEA recommends record 400M barrel oil release amid Iran war
item.description: The International Energy Agency has agreed to release a record 400 million barrels of oil.

item.title: Not ‘a litre of oil’ to pass Strait of Hormuz, expect $200 price tag: Iran
item.description: Warning comes as 400 million barrels of oil are being released from global reserves during waterway's closure.

item.title: US military confirms use of ‘advanced AI tools’ in war against Iran
item.description: Admiral Brad Cooper says artificial intelligence is helping process data, but humans are making final decisions.

item.title: Oil facilities in Oman’s Salalah port ablaze after drone strikes
item.description: Drones struck oil storage facilities in Oman’s ⁠Salalah port, as local authorities say they're responding to a big fire.

item.title: Could the US deploy troops to Iran, and how could that play out?
item.description: Experts say Iran’s vast, mountainous terrain would make invasion difficult but a small, precise mission is possible.
